name: agent-flow conformance vectors
note: public inputs -> expected recompute. 'conformant' = an impl reproduces every expected. The on-chain 8274 verify is a contract call, not a pure recompute, so it is checked separately (not a vector). Layer-3 bond-standing is a pure recompute over public survival(bondId) state, so it IS a vector; the live cast-call form is recompute-step scope/bond-standing. | v0.2: added 8312/budget-substrate vectors (IBudgetSubstrate: remaining = cap - spent, spent ≤ cap) — spec-formalized alias of the 8312/cap-conservation invariant. | v0.3: +8281/observation-digest (OCP digest = keccak256(observation), canonicalized from the Rust SDK's inline golden so all SDKs gate on one vector, not each impl's) + 8275 no-resolved-history edge (gw=gl=0 -> winRate null, fails-closed per the 'incomplete window' doctrine; surfaced porting the Rust compute_win_rate which returns None where the naive handler div-by-zero'd). | v0.4: +8275-reputation-rounding-tie (gw=1,gl=31 -> 1/32=0.03125 exact tie; pins half-away-from-zero as the shared rounding convention, matching Rust f64::round(), since Python's round() gives a different answer here -- the reference handler itself was hardened to Decimal+ROUND_HALF_UP in the same pass, not just the vector added). | v0.4: declared rounding_convention (canonical structured rule + content-hash, gated in the runner) + a fuzz family of 5 non-binary-exact 4dp ties (denom 800) that catch naive round()/f64::round which the binary-exact 1/32 tie passes — three green suites said nothing about a naive impl until this family. Basis-points is the endpoint (removes the rule by construction). | v0.5: +ens/contenthash (ENSIP-7 IPFS contenthash encoding = 0xe301 ‖ CID bytes) — the value the first ENS-write MCP sets via setContenthash; gates the ENS write leg's encoding, recomputable.

vectors.desc: keccak256(utf8(label)) — a name->handle binding (e.g. a CAIP agentRef as a preimage leg). NOT the ERC-8004 agentId (registry id) and NOT an ENS namehash.

vectors.spec: RAILS/ReceiptOS §2.8 — profile receiptos-c14n-v0 (JCS/RFC-8785 by reference + top-level anchor-strip as sole delta)
vectors.desc: receipt_root = 0x·sha256(C(strip_anchor(E))). The π forces LITERAL UTF-8 (non-ASCII NOT \u-escaped): canonical form is {"a":{"x":[true,null,"0x2a"],"y":"π"},"b":1} = 44 chars / 45 UTF-8 bytes. An impl that \u-escapes, mis-sorts keys, or strips more/less than the top-level anchor fails this vector. Verified byte-exact vs ReceiptOS v0.6 §2.8 (2026-07-11).
raw_input_hash = keccak256(raw_user_input)
sanitization_pipeline_hash = keccak256(utf8(cid) || raw_input_hash)
scopeRoot = keccak256(abi.encode(merkleRoot, count))

INITIAL-task taskHash, order+types confirmed vs filed erc-8301.md. GUARDS Fede's empty-array edge: prevReplyHashes empty → inner = keccak256(abi.encodePacked([])) = keccak256('')=0xc5d2…a470, NOT bytes32(0) — an impl that special-cases the empty array to 0x0 fails this vector.

the RECOMPUTABLE input: winRate = commit_gated_wins/(gated_wins+gated_losses) reproduces the ledger's published winRate (primary artifact). The composite reputation is an impl-defined VIEW, deliberately not a conformance value.
standing = (not slashed) and (not challenged) — recomputed from public bond state, not asserted
a slashed/challenged bond is NOT standing — its completeness claim was falsified
resolutionRoot = keccak256(abi.encode(leaves)); leaf = keccak256(abi.encode(bytes32 sourceId, uint8 option)); votes sorted by sourceId asc
same sourceIds, different values -> different root (so adversarial-a values can't reproduce the committed root)
single-source resolution
separated = w(a)!=w(b), b=a++[X]; 3-3-1 base + X(WIN) → 4-3-1 sole 50% flips → MATERIAL
3-3-1 base + X(LOSS) → 3-3-2 WIN/DRAW tie persists → NO_QUORUM → immaterial
6-1 WIN base + X(WIN) → 7-1 still WIN → not separated (robust majority, immaterial)

vectors.spec: ERC-8312 — bounded agent actions (cap invariant + Security clause: confirm/cancel bind to reserver)
vectors.desc: reserved+confirmed ≤ cap holds. Real captured state from Atlas-Protocol-AI/zero-human-loop @ 5530a9c, StatefulBound 0x3f79…faac, mandateId 0x9e3e…05bbe on Base Sepolia (storage-proven vs stateRoot).

vectors.spec: ERC-8312 — bounded agent actions (cap invariant, struct/turnover-cursor layout)
vectors.desc: Second independent implementation of the same invariant: orbmis/headroom CURSOR_HEADROOM (cumulativeTurnover ≤ maxCumulativeTurnover), distinct from StatefulBound's reserved+confirmed aggregate. Real captured state from EnvelopeRegistry 0x091f665B…07d1, envelope 0x48e972a2…bcb041 on Base Sepolia (84532), storage-proven vs stateRoot @ block 43697520 (0xbb4fbc63…) — cursorValues @ mapping-slot 2, agg=cumulativeTurnover(+2), cap=maxCumulativeTurnover(+1): recompute-step form `struct:2:2:1`.
synthetic over-cap control: aggregate exceeds the cap → the audit must reject (the cursor's enforce path makes this unreachable on-chain; the vector pins the verdict side).
namehash('eth') — the TLD node ENS resolves
namehash('agent.eth') — DISTINCT from keccak256(utf8('agent.eth'))=0x3ad7…; the ENS-identity fork Fede flagged on 8004/agent-id
namehash('vitalik.eth') — second 2-label vector
agentId = bytes32(uint256(registryId)); Wizgob = ERC-8004 registry id 860, left-padded
agentId = bytes32(uint256(registryId)); our dinamic.eth registry id 54848

vectors.spec: ERC-8275 §Composition-with-8294 seam 4 (envelope alignment); schemas pinned from ERC-8294 #1808 + ERC-8299/WYRIWE #1810
vectors.desc: One EIP-712 verifier recovers the signer for BOTH an ERC-8294 Attestation and a WYRIWE WyriweAttestation, rejects either when a committed field is tampered, and reconciles the shared agentId (8294 uint256 860 == WYRIWE bytes32(uint256(860))=0x..035c, the ERC-8299 rule). Signer = anvil key #1 (throwaway). Recompute via recompute-step seam4/envelope-align (node + ethers).

vectors.spec: ReceiptOS Step-5 capsule carrying the WYRIWE input_commitment slice; genesis encoding pinned. Three-case test vector authored by @pipavlo82, independently reproduced byte-identical (linked decision_sha256 rebuilt from the described body, not just the chaining arithmetic).
vectors.desc: input_commitment slice recomputes under wyriwe/raw + wyriwe/pipeline; capsule recomputes under receiptos/receipt-hash. Genesis: prev=null → prev_hex='' (empty), '|' delimiter retained. Linked: 2nd capsule (action.kind='nft_suggest_followup', decision.reason='input recomputes; policy clean; chained') chains onto genesis. Chain-tamper: flipping a prev_hex nibble MUST move the receipt.

verdictHash = keccak256(abi.encode(bytes32 jobId, keccak256(utf8 resultText))) — the commitment ConsultEscrow.release() recomputes on-chain before checking the attestor sig. Real mainnet settlement (job 0xbc01b40, release 0x779204, escrow 0x7057fbA7).

inputs.resultText: No intermediaries required, cryptographic verification only.

vectors.desc: IBudgetSubstrate: remaining MUST equal cap - spent while Active. cap=150, spent=60 → remaining=90; headroom holds. Formalizes the pre-standard StatefulBound (reserved+confirmed) / CursorHeadroom (aggregate) invariant into the spec's cap - spent = remaining semantics — same math, spec field names.
synthetic over-budget control: spent exceeds cap → the audit must reject. Mirrors cap-conservation-breach in the spent/cap field names.

vectors.spec: ERC-8312 — IBudgetSubstrate (remaining(id) is not trusted — recomputed)
vectors.desc: control for a lying substrate: it REPORTS remaining=100 but cap - spent = 90 → recompute rejects. remaining(id) is verified against cap - spent, never taken on trust.

digest = keccak256(observation) — the opaque OCP commitment record()s on-chain. Canonical vector so all SDKs gate on ONE digest, not each impl's inline golden (the Rust SDK carried this leg self-gated). observation = a real agentRef + action.

gated_wins=0, gated_losses=0 -> winRate is UNDEFINED (null), NOT 0.0. Fails-closed per the 'incomplete window' doctrine: a gap in evidence must never read as a clean zero record. Surfaced porting the Rust SDK compute_win_rate (returns None here) vs the naive handler which div-by-zero'd.

gw=1, gl=31 -> 1/32 = 0.03125 EXACTLY, a genuine tie at the 4th decimal. Pins the rounding convention every SDK must share: half-away-from-zero (matches Rust f64::round()), computed from exact integers -- NOT Python's round() (banker's/half-to-even, which gives 0.0312 here) and not naive float division (subject to binary representation error on top of the rounding-mode question). A naive float+round() implementation fails this vector even though it passes every other reputation vector, which is exactly why it needs its own pinned case rather than living in a code comment.

vectors.spec: ERC-8275 axis — rounding fuzz family (non-binary-exact tie)
vectors.desc: wins=57, losses=743 -> 57/800 = 0.07125, a 4th-decimal tie whose value isn't f64-representable (denominator has factor 5). Canonical ROUND_HALF_UP over exact rational = 0.0713; naive round() (banker's OR f64::round) gives 0.0712. Forces rounding_convention; catches naive round() that the binary-exact 1/32 tie cannot.

vectors.spec: ERC-8275 axis — rounding fuzz family (non-binary-exact tie)
vectors.desc: wins=69, losses=731 -> 69/800 = 0.08625, a 4th-decimal tie whose value isn't f64-representable (denominator has factor 5). Canonical ROUND_HALF_UP over exact rational = 0.0863; naive round() (banker's OR f64::round) gives 0.0862. Forces rounding_convention; catches naive round() that the binary-exact 1/32 tie cannot.

vectors.spec: ERC-8275 axis — rounding fuzz family (non-binary-exact tie)
vectors.desc: wins=113, losses=687 -> 113/800 = 0.14125, a 4th-decimal tie whose value isn't f64-representable (denominator has factor 5). Canonical ROUND_HALF_UP over exact rational = 0.1413; naive round() (banker's OR f64::round) gives 0.1412. Forces rounding_convention; catches naive round() that the binary-exact 1/32 tie cannot.

vectors.spec: ERC-8275 axis — rounding fuzz family (non-binary-exact tie)
vectors.desc: wins=139, losses=661 -> 139/800 = 0.17375, a 4th-decimal tie whose value isn't f64-representable (denominator has factor 5). Canonical ROUND_HALF_UP over exact rational = 0.1738; naive round() (banker's OR f64::round) gives 0.1737. Forces rounding_convention; catches naive round() that the binary-exact 1/32 tie cannot.

vectors.spec: ERC-8275 axis — rounding fuzz family (non-binary-exact tie)
vectors.desc: wins=163, losses=637 -> 163/800 = 0.20375, a 4th-decimal tie whose value isn't f64-representable (denominator has factor 5). Canonical ROUND_HALF_UP over exact rational = 0.2038; naive round() (banker's OR f64::round) gives 0.2037. Forces rounding_convention; catches naive round() that the binary-exact 1/32 tie cannot.

contenthash = 0xe301 ‖ CID bytes (ENSIP-7 IPFS namespace). The value the first ENS-write MCP sets via setContenthash(node, hash) so a name resolves an IPFS site through eth.limo. CIDv1 base32 (b-prefix) → strip multibase, decode base32, prefix 0xe301. Recomputable + hand-verifiable.

rounding_convention.note: Exact integer/rational division internally; round only at the output boundary, half-away-from-zero (ROUND_HALF_UP over exact Decimal); never a language float round(). Conformance gates on this hash, not on whichever ties are in the vector set. See CONVENTIONS.md. Basis-points is the destination (removes the rule by construction).
